### Post by McTek on 2007-08-11
After running NTFS-3g my vfat partition is no longer accessable on my network nor can I mount it from linux without entering my password and there is no longer an entry in the fstab file.

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.

This is what fstab looks looks like now. Notice there is no entry for the vfat partition.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda10 :
UUID=a71b7121-3160-4716-9ca4-ad14d2a2aa40 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda11 :
UUID=2c5c2de0-39a9-4a53-ade5-ddb7a2867074 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hda1 /media/winxp ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0

---

### Post by meierfra on 2007-08-11
Type 
```
sudo fdisk -l
```

in a terminal and post the output.

### Post by meierfra on 2007-08-11
Create a directory with a name of your choice in  /media:

```
sudo mkdir /media/name_of_your_choice
```

open /etc/fstab via:

```
gksudo gedit /etc/fstab
```

add the following line at the end:

/dev/hda5   /media/name_of_your_choice  vfat  iocharset=utf8,umask=000  0    0

Save the file and reboot.

---


add the following line at the end:

/dev/hda5   /media/name_of_your_choice  vfat  iocharset=utf8,umask=000  0    0

Save the file and reboot.

How were you able to determine the form of the entry for fstab from the information that the listing of the partitions gave you?
 Many thanks!

### Post by meierfra on 2007-08-11
The only information I got from your "fdisk" was    "/dev/hda5".  The rest is the standard way to mount a fat32 partition:

[http://ubuntuguide.org/wiki/Ubuntu_dapper#How_to_mount_Windows_partitions_.28FAT.29_on_boot-up.2C_and_allow_all_users_to_read.2Fwrite]("http://ubuntuguide.org/wiki/Ubuntu_dapper#How_to_mount_Windows_partitions_.28FAT.29_on_boot-up.2C_and_allow_all_users_to_read.2Fwrite")
